mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption demand, which raises expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate persistently above 8.5% indicates substantial labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an unemployment rate above 8% and a federal funds rate below 0.25% signals a zero-bound policy response to labor market distress, where expected future accommodation compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy with labor slack
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of UNRATE > 8% and FEDFUNDS < 0.25% fail to produce positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over the main effects.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery, a sustained three-month acceleration in industrial production signals rebounding manufacturing activity and inventory rebuilding, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months classified as post-crisis recovery, three-month acceleration in INDPRO coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3% signals rising inflationary pressure that tightens real monetary conditions and erodes margin expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases above 0.3% are followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls jointly signal strengthening construction demand and labor income, reinforcing aggregate demand expansion and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in HOUST and PAYEMS do not produce positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of concurrent observations, or the interaction term coefficient is not positive and significant (p > 0.05) in a linear specification.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls signals contracting labor income and weakening aggregate demand, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in PAYEMS are followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the mean 5-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a falling unemployment expansion, month-over-month growth in retail sales signals strengthening consumer demand that outpaces labor market gains and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods of falling UNRATE, month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
